info.description: A comprehensive development toolkit for Odoo environments with profiling, monitoring, and optimization features.
info.title: OdooDevTools API

post.description:
Agent endpoint: receive an aggregated batch of ORM stats and critical events.
The batch is pushed onto a Redis stream for asynchronous processing by the ingest worker.
Supports gzip-compressed bodies (Content-Encoding: gzip). Max body size: 5 MB.
Agent endpoint: receive a batch of Odoo error/exception events
Agent endpoint: persist a collected Odoo schema snapshot

get.description: Upgrades the HTTP connection to a WebSocket connection and handles agent heartbeats.

400.description: Bad Request: Missing or invalid agent_id

401.description: Unauthorized: Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden: Agent does not belong to the tenant

404.description: Not Found: Environment not found for the given agent_id

500.description: Internal Server Error: Failed to upgrade connection or database error

post.description: Lets a logged-in user change their own password. Requires the current password for verification. All sessions are revoked on success.
Always returns HTTP 200 regardless of whether the email is registered (prevents user enumeration). Sends a reset link via MailHog when the account exists.
Authenticates a user by email and password. Enforces brute-force protection via Redis. Returns an access and refresh token pair.
Invalidates the current session or all sessions when logout_all=true. Requires a valid Bearer token.

get.description: Returns the profile of the authenticated user.

patch.description: Updates full_name and/or email for the authenticated user. If the email is changed a new verification email is sent via MailHog.

post.description: Rotates the refresh token (old one is blacklisted in Redis) and issues a new access + refresh pair.
Creates a new tenant and owner user, and returns an access and refresh token.
Sends a fresh verification email via MailHog. Returns 409 if the email is already verified.
Consumes the one-time Redis token and updates the user's password. All existing sessions are revoked on success.

get.description: Returns all active sessions for the authenticated user. Redis is checked first; the DB is used as a fallback.

delete.description: Invalidates a specific session by ID. The session must belong to the authenticated user.

post.description: Consumes the one-time Redis verification token and marks the user's email as verified in the database.

get.description: Get all environments for the current tenant

post.description: Register a new Odoo environment under the current tenant

get.description: Get details of a specific environment

delete.description: Permanently delete an environment and its associated data

post.description: Generate a one-time token that an agent uses to self-register and obtain API credentials

get.description: Return all API keys for the given environment. Raw key values are never included.

post.description: Generate a new agent API key for the given environment. The raw key is returned once and never shown again. If expires_at is omitted the key expires in 90 days. If scopes is omitted it defaults to ["agent:write"].

delete.description: Mark an API key as inactive. The agent using it will receive 401 on the next request.

get.description: Returns aggregated error groups for the given environment, sorted by occurrence count

parameters.description: Filter by status: open, acknowledged, resolved
parameters.name: status

get.description: Returns a single error group with optional raw traceback from S3

parameters.description: Include raw traceback from S3 (default false)
parameters.name: trace

put.description: Update feature flags for an environment and push to connected agent

get.description: Return schema snapshot summaries for the given environment, newest first
Return the most recently captured schema snapshot for the given environment
Filter models in the latest snapshot by technical name or display label

raw_key.description: shown once; not stored in plaintext

sample_sql.example: SELECT id, name FROM res_partner WHERE active = true

expires_at.description: RFC3339; defaults to 90 days from now if omitted

session_id.description: specific session; empty = current token only

ApiKeyAuth.description: Type "ApiKey" followed by a space and your API key (e.g. "ApiKey odt_xxx...").
ApiKeyAuth.name: Authorization

BearerAuth.description: Type "Bearer" followed by a space and JWT token.
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
